skill.name: basecamp-automation
skill.description: Automate Basecamp project management, to-dos, messages, people, and to-do list organization via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Basecamp workflows including task management, message posting, and team coordination through Rube MCP integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 225 pattern matches but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation (Markdown format), not executable code. Backticks are inline code formatting for API tool names, not shell execution. One legitimate network dependency on rube.app MCP server for Basecamp API access. Safe for publication with user awareness of external service dependency.

low_findings.title: External MCP Service Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires connection to external Rube MCP server at https://rube.app/mcp for Basecamp API access. Users should trust this third-party service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Explicit documentation states the MCP endpoint URL as a required dependency for skill functionality.

content.value_statement: Streamline Basecamp workflows by automating to-do lists, messages, and team management. Eliminate repetitive manual tasks through AI-driven Basecamp API operations.
actual_capabilities: Create and organize to-do lists within Basecamp projects using API tool sequences
Post and update messages on project message boards with HTML-formatted content
Manage team members by listing people and controlling project access permissions
Organize to-dos into color-coded groups for better task categorization
Browse projects and retrieve hierarchical ID structures for downstream operations
Resolve Basecamp IDs through systematic top-down lookup patterns
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP server connection at https://rube.app/mcp
Basecamp connection must be authenticated via OAuth before any operations
All rich text fields require HTML format, not Markdown
Subject to Basecamp API rate limits for sequential requests

use_cases.title: Project Manager Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Automatically create structured to-do lists for new projects, assign tasks to team members with due dates, and post kickoff messages to keep everyone aligned.

use_cases.title: Team Onboarding Streamlining
use_cases.description: Bulk add new team members to relevant projects, create onboarding task lists with sequential dependencies, and send welcome messages with resource links.
use_cases.target_user: HR coordinators and team leads managing new hires

use_cases.title: Sprint Planning Assistant
use_cases.description: Create sprint to-do groups organized by priority colors, populate with user stories and acceptance criteria, and notify stakeholders of sprint kickoff.
use_cases.target_user: Agile teams running iterative development cycles

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a to-do list called "Week 1 Tasks" in the Marketing Campaign project with three tasks: draft copy, design graphics, and schedule posts. Assign all tasks to the marketing team with a due date of next Friday.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick task list creation for a specific project

prompt_templates.title: Message Board Announcement
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a message to the Engineering project board with subject "Sprint 24 Kickoff" and content announcing the sprint goals, key deadlines, and linking to the planning document. Notify only the tech leads.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted project announcements with selective notifications

prompt_templates.title: Team Access Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Add three new team members to the Product Launch project: Alice (alice@company.com), Bob (bob@company.com), and Carol (carol@company.com). Then list all current project members to confirm access.
prompt_templates.scenario: Bulk team member onboarding with verification

prompt_templates.title: Complex Project Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a complete project structure: create to-do groups for Planning, Execution, and Review with green, yellow, and red colors respectively. Add five tasks distributed across groups with assignees and due dates. Post a kickoff message summarizing the plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end project initialization with multiple operations

output_examples.input: Create to-do list "Q1 Goals" in project 12345
output_examples.output: To-do list created successfully: Q1 Goals (ID: 67890). Access at https://basecamp.com/12345/todolists/67890
Project members (5): John Smith (john@company.com), Jane Doe (jane@company.com), Bob Wilson (bob@company.com), Alice Brown (alice@company.com), Carol Davis (carol@company.com)

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to retrieve current API schemas before executing operations
Verify Basecamp connection status shows ACTIVE before running any workflows to avoid authentication errors
Return app_url links from API responses to users instead of raw IDs for better usability
anti_patterns: Creating duplicate to-do lists without checking existing lists first by name
Using status=draft for messages which may cause HTTP 400 errors - always use status=active
Manually constructing Basecamp URLs from IDs instead of using app_url from API responses

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and how do I set it up?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server that provides Basecamp API access. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your Claude Code configuration. No API keys are required - just add the endpoint.

faq.question: Why do I need to call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first?
faq.answer: Tool schemas may change over time. Calling RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS ensures you have the current parameter requirements and available tools before executing any Basecamp operations.

faq.question: What is the difference between bucket_id and project_id?
faq.answer: They refer to the same entity. Different Basecamp API endpoints use different parameter names - bucket_id for some operations, project_id for others. The numeric value is identical.

faq.question: Can I use Markdown in message and to-do descriptions?
faq.answer: No, Basecamp only accepts HTML formatting. Use tags like <strong>, <em>, <ul>, <li>, and <a> for rich text. Markdown will not be rendered correctly.

faq.question: How do I find the correct todoset_id or message_board_id?
faq.answer: Check the project's dock array returned by BASECAMP_GET_PROJECT or use dedicated lookup tools like BASECAMP_GET_BUCKETS_TODOSETS and BASECAMP_GET_MESSAGE_BOARD.

faq.question: What happens if I hit Basecamp's rate limits?
faq.answer: Basecamp may return HTTP 429 errors. Space out rapid sequential requests and implement retry logic with exponential backoff for production workflows.
